FIRST AMENDMENT TO TRUST AGREEMENT

THIS AGREEMENT, made this 7th day of January,
1963, by and between JOSABEL L. READ, of Shenandoah, Iowa,
hereinafter called the "Settlor," and the Settlor's daughter,
MARION R. AMBLER, of Waco, Texas, hereinafter called the
"Trustee,"

W I T N E S S E T H:

WHEREAS, on June 1, 1952, the Settlor and the Trustee
entered into a certain revocable Trust Agreement for the benefit
of the beneficiaries therein named, said Agreement being known
as "The Josabel L. Read Trust," and

WHEREAS, Article XV of said Trust Agreement provided
in part as follows:

"Settlor • • • declares that this trust shall be
revocable at will and that Settlor shall have full power
• • • (b) at any time or from time to time by an instrument in writing delivered during the Settlor's lifetime
to the Trustee, to terminate this Agreement in whole or
in part, or to modify or amend it, provided, that in the
case of modification, or amendment, the duties, powers
and liabilities of the Trustee shall not be charged without their written consent • • •," and

WHEREAS, the Settlor desires to amend the terms of said
Trust Agreement and the Trustee hereby consents to said amendment;

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the premises and
pursuant to the right of amendment set forth in said Trust Agreement, it is mutually agreed by and between the Settlor and the
Trustee as follows:

ITEM I

Articles III, IV, V, VI, VII, VIII, IX, X, XI, XII,

XIII, XIV, XV, XVI, XVII and XVIII of said Trust Agreement are

hereby cancelled and revoked and in lieu thereof the following

Articles III, IV, V, VI, VII, VIII, IX, X, XI, XII, XIII, XIV,

XV, XVI, XVII, XVIII, XIX, XX and XXI are hereby substituted:

ARTICLE III

During the lifetime of the Settlor, the trust estate

and all income derived therefrom shall be held, administered and

distributed as follows:

A. The Trustee shall, at convenient intervals but

not less frequently than quarter-annually, pay to or use for the

benefit of the Settlor all of the net income derived from the

trust estate so long as she shall live. Upon the death of the

Settlor any accrued or collected but undistributed net income

which she would have otherwise been entitled to receive shall

not be distributed to the executors or administrators of her

estate but instead shall be treated as part of the principal of

the trust estate.

B. The Settlor shall have the absolute right to with-

draw from the trust estate such sum or sums out of the principal

thereof as she may designate by written request to the Trustee.

If such withdrawals shall exhaust the trust estate, then the

trust estate shall terminate upon such exhaustion. Any with-

drawals so made by the Settlor shall not be deemed to be advance-

ments upon any payments of net income or principal which the

Settlor may thereafter become entitled to receive.

C. If at any time or times during the life of the

Settlor, the Trustee, in her sole and absolute discretion, should

conclude that further funds are needed for the comfortable

- 2 -

the other of which shall be named for and set aside for the
benefit of the Settlor's daughter, [illegible], and
shall be designated as the "[illegible]Share." Each
of such separate shares shall be held and administered as a
separate and distinct trust for all purposes.

C. The Marion Read Ambler Share, the [illegible]
[illegible] Share and the [illegible] Share of the trust
estate shall each be held, administered and distributed as
follows:

1. From the time the trust estate is divided into
shares, the Trustee shall pay to the daughter of the
Settlor for whom said share is named, at convenient
intervals but not less frequently than quarter-annually,
as much of the net income derived from said share as the
Trustee, in her sole and absolute discretion, shall determine and direct. Any and all income not so paid to
the daughter of the Settlor for whom said share is named
shall be added to the principal of said share, or paid
to any one or more of the children or grandchildren of
the daughter of the Settlor for whom said share is named
who are living at the time of payment of said net income
in whatever proportions that the Trustee, in her sole and
absolute discretion, shall determine and direct.

2. Upon the death of the daughter of the Settlor
for whom said share is named (provided she is living at
the time of the Settlor's death), the Trustee shall distribute all the property then constituting said share (or
any part thereof specified by said daughter) to or for the
benefit of such persons or organizations in such proportions
and subject to such trusts, powers and conditions as said
daughter may provide and appoint by Will; provided, however,
that said daughter shall not have the power to appoint to
or for the benefit of herself, her estate or the creditors
of either, and provided further, that no trust created
under the foregoing special testamentary power of appointment granted to said daughter shall last longer than
twenty-one (21) years after the death of the last to die
of the Settlor's daughter, MARION READ AMBLER, the Settlor's
daughter, [illegible], the Settlor's daughter,
[illegible], the Settlor's grandson, [illegible],
the Settlor's granddaughter, [illegible], the
Settlor's grandson, [illegible], the
Settlor's granddaughter, [illegible], the
Settlor's granddaughter, [illegible], the Settlor's
grandson, [illegible], and the Settlor's grandson, READ
AMBLER. The Trustee shall be fully protected in relying

- 4 -

maintenance and support of the Settlor, taking into consideration
any other resources available to the Settlor of which the Trustee
has actual knowledge, the Trustee is authorized and empowered to
pay to or use for the benefit of the Settlor such sum or sums out
of the principal of the trust estate as the Trustee may deem
necessary. Any sums so paid to or used for the benefit of the
Settlor shall not be deemed to be advancements upon any payments
of net income which she may thereafter become entitled to receive.

ARTICLE IV

Upon the death of the Settlor, the trust estate (in-
cluding the proceeds of any insurance on the life of the Settlor
which may be payable to the Trustee hereunder) and all income
derived therefrom shall be held, administered and distributed as
follows:

A. The Trustee shall distribute all automobiles and
accessories thereto, jewelry, clothing, purely personal effects,
chinaware, silverware, books, paintings, objects of art, furni-
ture and household goods and furnishings of every kind and char-
acter constituting part of the trust estate in equal shares to
those of the Settlor's three daughters, MARION READ AMBLER,
[illegible] and [illegible], who survive her.

B. The Trustee shall divide all of the rest, residue
and remainder of the property then constituting the trust estate
into three shares of equal value, one of which shall be named
for and set aside for the benefit of the Settlor's daughter,
MARION READ AMBLER, and shall be designated as the "Marion Read
Ambler Share," one of which shall be named for and set aside
for the benefit of the Settlor's daughter, [illegible],
and shall be designated as "[illegible] Share," and

- 3 -

upon an instrument which has been admitted to probate
in any jurisdiction is the will of said daughter, or if
the Trustee has no actual notice of the existence of a
will of said daughter within three (3) months after her
death, the Trustee may conclusively presume said daughter
died without exercising said special testamentary power
of appointment.

3. If the daughter of the Settlor for whom said share
is named should not survive the Settlor, or if she should
survive the Settlor but shall fail to execute the special
testamentary power of appointment granted to her in sub-
paragraph 2 of this paragraph C, or if her exercise thereof
should be invalid or should for any reason whatsoever fail
to take effect, or if her exercise thereof should be with
reference to only part of the property constituting said
share at the time of her death, then upon the death of
the last to die of said daughter and the Settlor the
Trustee shall divide all of the property then constituting
said share with respect to which said special testamentary
power of appointment (a) was not exercised, or (b) was
exercised but failed to take effect, into subshares of
such number and value as would be required if said prop-
erty were then to be divided among said daughter's issue
then living, per stirpes, but instead of so distributing
said property the Trustee shall set aside and designate
each such subshare as a separate trust fund for the bene-
fit of the beneficiary who would have received such sub-
share if said property had been so distributed, and the
Trustee shall thereafter hold, administer and distribute
each such subshare in the manner provided in paragraph D
of this Article IV. If, however, there should be no issue
of said daughter living at the time when said share is to
be divided into subshares then the trust with respect to
said share shall terminate upon the death of the last to
die of said daughter and the Settlor, and the Trustee
shall thereupon distribute all of the property constitu-
ting said share to the Settlor's issue then living, per
stirpes; provided, however, that if any property would
thus become distributable to any beneficiary for whose
benefit the Trustee is then holding or is then to hold a
separate share or subshare of the trust estate then such
property shall not be distributed to such beneficiary but
instead shall be added to and thereafter be administered
and distributed as a part of such other separate share or
subshare.

D. Each subshare of any share of the trust estate shall
be held, administered and distributed as follows:

1. If the beneficiary for whose benefit said subshare
was set aside is younger than twenty-one (21) years of age
at the time said subshare is to be set aside then until
said beneficiary attains the age of twenty-one (21) years
(or until the death of said beneficiary if he or she should

- 5 -

die prior to attaining said age) the Trustee shall pay
to or use for the benefit of said beneficiary as much of
the net income derived from said subshare as the Trustee
may deem necessary or proper for the comfortable main-
tenance, support and education of said beneficiary, taking
into consideration any other resources available to said
beneficiary of which the Trustee has actual knowledge.
Any net income not so paid to or used for the benefit of
said beneficiary shall be added to the principal of said
subshare at such times as the Trustee may deem appropriate;
provided, however, that at the time said beneficiary at-
tains the age of twenty-one (21) years any collected but
undistributed net income shall immediately be added to the
principal of said subshare, and any accrued but uncollected
net income shall be treated as income derived from said
subshare after said beneficiary attains the age of twenty-
one (21) years.

2. From the time the beneficiary for whose benefit
said subshare was set aside attains the age of twenty-one
(21) years [or from the time said subshare is to be set
aside if said beneficiary has then already attained said
age] until the termination of the trust with respect to
said subshare as hereinafter provided, the Trustee shall
pay to said beneficiary, at convenient intervals but not
less frequently than quarter-annually, all of the net
income derived from said subshare.

3. If said subshare has not already terminated as
hereinafter provided then when the beneficiary for whose
benefit said subshare was set aside attains the age of
thirty (30) years (or at the time said subshare is to be
set aside if said beneficiary has then already attained
said age) the Trustee shall distribute to said beneficiary
one-third (1/3) of the property then constituting said
subshare.

4. If said subshare has not already terminated as
hereinafter provided, then when the beneficiary for whose
benefit said subshare was set aside attains the age of
thirty-five (35) years (or at the time said subshare is to
be set aside if said beneficiary has then already attained
said age) the Trustee shall distribute to said beneficiary
an additional one-half (1/2) of the property then consti-
tuting said subshare.

5. The trust with respect to said subshare shall
terminate either (a) when the beneficiary for whose bene-
fit said subshare was set aside attains the age of forty
(40) years if he or she has not yet attained said age at
the time said subshare is to be set aside, or (b) at the
time said subshare is to be set aside if said beneficiary
has then already attained the age of forty (40) years, or
(c) upon the death of said beneficiary if he or she should
die prior to attaining the age of forty (40) years, or
(d) upon the expiration of twenty-one (21) years after the

- 6 -

death of the last to die of the Settlor's daughter, MARION
BAD AMBLER, the Settlor's daughter, [illegible], the Settlor's
daughter, [illegible], the Settlor's
granddaughter, [illegible], the Settlor's granddaughter,
[illegible], the Settlor's granddaughter, [illegible]
the Settlor's grandson, [illegible]; the Settlor's
grandson, [illegible]) the Settlor's grandson,
READ AMBLER, and the Settlor, whichever of said four events
shall first occur.

6. Upon the termination of the trust with respect to
said subshare the Trustee shall distribute all of the prop-
erty then constituting said subshare to the beneficiary
for whose benefit said subshare was set aside if he or she
is living at the time of the termination of the trust with
respect to said subshare, but if said beneficiary should
not be then living then to the issue then living of said
beneficiary, per stirpes, but if there should also be no
issue of said beneficiary then living then to the issue
then living of said beneficiary's nearest
ancestor who was a lineal descendant of the Settlor and
has issue then living, but if there should also be no
issue then living of any such ancestor of said beneficiary
then to the Settlor's issue then living, per stirpes;
provided, however, that if under any of the provisions
contained in this subparagraph 6 any property would be-
come distributable to any beneficiary for whose benefit
the Trustee is then holding a separate share or subshare
of the trust estate then such property shall not be dis-
tributed to such beneficiary but instead shall be added
to and thereafter be administered and distributed as a
part of such other separate share or subshare.

E. In the event there should be no issue of the Settlor
living at the time the trust estate is to be divided into shares,
or in the event there should be no issue of the Settlor living
at the time of the termination of the last remaining share or
subshare of the trust estate, then in either of such events the
entire trust shall terminate, and the Trustee shall thereupon
distribute all of the property then constituting the trust estate
to those persons who, according to the statutes of descents and
distributions of the State of Iowa then in effect and in the pro-
portions provided by said laws, would have been entitled to in-
herit the Settlor's estate if she had died intestate at the time

- 7 -

of the termination of the trust.

F. If at any time or times during the existence of
any share of the trust estate and prior to the time said share
is to be divided into subshares the Trustee should conclude that
further funds are needed for the comfortable maintenance, support
or education of the daughter of the Settlor for whom said share
is named or for the comfortable maintenance, support or education
of any issue of said daughter, taking into consideration any other
resources available to said daughter or such issue of which the
Trustee has actual knowledge, the Trustee is authorized and em-
powered to pay to or use for the benefit of said daughter or
such issue such sum or sums out of the principal of said share
as the Trustee may deem necessary. Any sums so paid to or used
for the benefit of said daughter shall not be deemed to be ad-
vancements upon any payments of net income which she may there-
after become entitled or eligible to receive, and any sums so
paid to or used for the benefit of any issue of said daughter
shall not be deemed to be advancements upon any subshare there-
after set aside.

G. If at any time or times during the existence of
any subshare of the trust estate the Trustee should conclude that
further funds are needed for the comfortable maintenance, support
or education of the beneficiary for whose benefit said subshare
was set aside, taking into consideration any other resources
available to said beneficiary of which the Trustee has actual
knowledge, the Trustee is authorized and empowered to pay to or
use for the benefit of said beneficiary such sum or sums out of
the principal of said subshare as the Trustee may deem necessary.
Any sums so paid to or used for the benefit of said beneficiary

- 8 -

shall not be deemed to be advancements upon any payments of net
income which said beneficiary may thereafter become entitled to
receive.

H. The power granted by paragraphs F and G of this
Article IV is purely discretionary in the Trustee and shall not
create any legal or equitable right or power whatsoever in any
beneficiary; and, accordingly, while any beneficiary of the trust
estate is acting as a Trustee hereunder the other Trustees (or
the other Trustee if there should then be only one other Trustee)
shall have the sole power to determine whether any discretionary
income or principal payment to or for the benefit of said beneficiary shall be made, and said beneficiary shall have no power
to make or to participate in making such determination.

I. Upon the death of any beneficiary who is entitled
to receive net income from the trust estate or for whose benefit
a share or subshare of the trust estate is being held, any undistributed net income derived from the trust estate or from
said share or subshare shall not be distributed to the executors
or administrators of the estate of said beneficiary but intend
any of said undistributed net income which has already been collected shall be added to the principal of the trust estate or
said share or subshare and any of said undistributed net income which has accrued but has not yet been collected shall be
treated as income to the next succeeding beneficial interests
or interest.

- 9 -

ARTICLE V

Wherever the issue living at any particular time of
any person (including the Settlor) are referred to in this Trust
Agreement, the term "issue" shall be deemed to mean all of such
person's lineal descendants of every degree living at that time
who have no ancestor then living who is a lineal descendant of
such person. The term "children" as used in this Trust Agreement
shall be deemed to mean lineal descendants of the Settlor's
daughters, MARION READ AMBLER, [illegible] and [illegible]
[illegible], of the first degree only, and the term "grandchildren"
shall be deemed to be lineal descendants of the Settlor's said
daughters of the second and third degrees only. A lineal de-
scendant shall be deemed to be living from the time of conception
provided such lineal descendant is subsequently born alive, but
if not born alive such lineal descendant shall be deemed never to
have been living. Wherever used in this Trust Agreement (in-
cluding any of the provisions of this Article V) the term "lineal
descendant" shall be deemed to include legally adopted persons
and their lineal descendants, and the term "ancestor" shall be
deemed to mean a lineal ascendant of any degree, by blood or
adoption.

ARTICLE VI

The Trustee shall have all the powers generally con-
ferred upon trustees by law and, without limiting such powers
but in furtherance thereof and in addition thereto, the Trustee

- 10 -

is hereby authorized and empowered to:

A. Hold, manage, operate, maintain and control the
property constituting the trust estate according to her
own judgment and discretion; invest and reinvest and keep
the trust estate invested in any form of property, or in
any manner, which she deems advantageous to the trust
estate, without restriction and with no obligation to
keep the trust estate invested in the type of property
designated by law or custom as legal for the investment
of trust funds or to retain and continue to hold indefinitely
any real or personal property deposited hereunder or
acquired by the Trustee from any source, regardless of
whether such property is of the kind designated by law
or custom as legal for the investment of trust funds or is
productive of income, and even though the retention thereof
results in a lack of diversification;

B. Take title to any property, real or personal, or
cause securities or other property to be registered, in
her name as Trustee hereunder, or in her own name, or in
the name of a nominee without disclosing the trust, or in
the case of securities to take and keep them unregistered
and in such condition that they will pass by delivery;

C. Determine in such manner as she deems just all
questions of trust accounting which appear to the Trustee
to be doubtful and, upon such determination, to allocate
to or apportion between principal and income, in such
manner as she deems just, any money or property at any
time received or disbursed by her, including but not
limited to premiums and discounts, the proceeds of extraordinary cash dividends or of stock dividends, the proceeds of the sale of property which has been unproductive
in whole or in part, receipts from wasting assets, and
the proceeds of the sale of property coming to her by or
in lieu of foreclosure or in liquidation; allocate any
particular property or an undivided interest therein to
any share or subshare of the trust estate or to any distributee of any part of the trust estate, make distributions of principal or income, or both, either in cash
or in any other property of equal value, or partly in
cash and partly in such other property, and determine
without formal appraisal the value of property so allocated or distributed; take and rely upon, for her full
protection when acting in good faith, advice of competent
counsel concerning any question of construction of this
Trust Agreement or any question with respect to the administration or distribution of the trust estate;

D. Vote in person or by proxy (general or special)
all shares of stock and other securities of any kind at
any time held by her; become a party to voting trust
agreements, initiate or participate at any stage in any
plan for the merger, consolidation, reorganization, recapitalization, financing, refinancing, or liquidation
of any corporation, association or other organization of

- 11 -

which she holds shares, obligations or other securities,
and also to that end to deposit such property, either condi-
tionally or unconditionally, with stockholders' or bond-
holders' protective committees and to institute suits,
pay assessments, costs, and expenses, either in advance
or otherwise, for the protection of her interests or en-
forcement of her rights as Trustee hereunder; exercise any
conversion or subscription rights pertaining to any stocks,
bonds, or other securities held hereunder;

E. Settle, compromise, adjust, liquidate or release
claims and debts in favor of or against the trust; accept
conveyances in lieu of foreclosure and take, hold and
retain, without regard to productivity of income or the
quality or kind thereof, any property coming to her by or
in lieu of foreclosure, or through complete or partial
liquidation of any corporation, or as a result of any
adjustment of accounts or arrearages; purchase at public
or private sale any property in which she has any interest
as Trustee hereunder, reduce interest rates on obligations
owing to the trust, release guaranties and accept sur-
render of leases, and extend mortgages or carry them past
due without extension and without regard to the value of the
security, for a consideration or gratuitously if she deems
it advisable;

F. Employ agents or attorneys; pay insurance prem-
iums, assessments on shares of stock, expenses of refi-
nancing or reorganizations, taxes and general or special
assessments; insure against all risks of loss, including
public liability, make ordinary and extraordinary repairs,
improvements and rebuilding improvements on any property
hereunder held in freehold or leasehold; raze existing
buildings or other improvements and, if the Trustee deems
it advisable, erect new buildings or other improvements
in lieu thereof;

G. Distribute income or principal, or both (including
final distribution), direct to any minor beneficiary, or
to the natural guardian of such minor, or to any person
or person having custody of such minor or with whom such
minor may be residing, or use or apply such income or
principal directly for the benefit of such minor, if the
Trustee in her sole and absolute discretion deems any of
said methods of distribution advisable, and upon procuring
the receipt of such minor or of any other person to whom
distribution is made, or of the Trustee shall be
fully discharged and released from all liability with re-
spect to the property so distributed; require the appoint-
ment of a legal guardian or curator, or both, for any
minor distributee if she deems it advisable for any reason;

H. Sell or exchange any property, real or personal,
constituting all or part of the trust estate, either for
cash or upon deferred payments, with or without taking a
lien or other security for the purchase money; lease for
terms of ninety-nine years or less; grant options, easements

- 12 -

and oil, gas and mineral rights in perpetuity or for such
times and upon such terms and conditions, and when and
as often, as the Trustee deems advisable; dedicate or grant
public easements, for a consideration or gratuitously, if
she deems such action advisable; develop and improve any
properties containing oil, gas or other minerals and engage
in exploring for oil, gas and other minerals; pay delay
rentals on leases for such periods as the Trustee deems
advisable; abandon property deemed by the Trustee to be
burdensome or valueless; redeem any property which the
Settlor may have pledged, mortgaged, or in any manner
hypothecated; borrow money, and mortgage or pledge trust
property as security, solely in her capacity as Trustee
and without individual liability; institute proceedings
for the partition of any or all property, real or per-
sonal, in which the Trustee holds any interest hereunder,
and bid at any partition sales for and on behalf of the
trust and use any or all cash or other property held by
her hereunder for the purpose of purchasing property at
partition sales;

I. Advance funds from time to time as additional
margin and pledge any part of the trust estate to prevent
the loss of any common or preferred stock which the Settlor
may have purchased on margin, or complete the purchase of
or sell any such stock;

J. Take out policies of insurance on the life or
lives of any beneficiary of the trust, such policies of
insurance to be of any type of life, endowment or annuity
insurance (including term and decreasing term) as the
Trustee may select, and the trust shall be the beneficiary
of any such policies acquired;

K. Continue and carry on any farming operation in
which the trust estate or any share or subshare thereof
may be financially interested and operate any other farm
which may be acquired and, in so doing by way of illustra-
tion and not limitation of her powers, to operate the farm
with hired labor, tenants or share croppers and to employ
agents; lease or rent the farm for cash or for a share of
the crops; purchase or otherwise acquire farm machinery
and equipment and livestock; construct, repair, and im-
prove farm buildings of all sorts needed, in her judgment,
for the operation of the farm; make or obtain loans or
advances at the prevailing rate or rates of interest for
farm purposes such as for production, harvesting, or
marketing, or for the construction, repair or improvement
of farm buildings, or for the purchase of farm machinery
or equipment or livestock; employ approved soil conserva-
tion practices in order to conserve, improve, and maintain
the fertility and the productivity of the soil; protect,
manage, and improve the timber and forest on the farm and
sell the timber and forest products when it is to the
best interest of the trust estate; ditch and drain damp or
wet field areas of the farm when and where needed; engage
in livestock production, if it is deemed advisable, and
construct such fences and buildings and plant such pastures

- 13 -

and crops as may be necessary to carry on such a livestock
program; and, in general, employ the methods of carrying
on the farming operation that are in common use by other
landowners in the community in which the farm is located.

L. Make, execute and deliver or accept all deeds,
leases, mortgages, assignments, notes, checks, endorsements,
consents, releases and all other instruments necessary or
convenient in exercising the powers and performing the
duties of the Trustee.

ARTICLE VII

Since the primary concern of the Settlor is for the
welfare of the immediate beneficiaries of the trust rather than
for the remote beneficiaries the Settlor directs the Trustee to
administer the trust estate from time to time to the best ad-
vantage of the beneficiary or beneficiaries then entitled to
enjoy the benefits of the trust estate, even though the result
might be detrimental to subsequent beneficiaries. Accordingly,
the Trustee shall be under no duty to create out of income any
reserve for depreciation or depletion. If, however, the Trustee,
in her sole and absolute discretion, deems it advisable, she is
authorized to create reserves for depreciation or depletion.

ARTICLE VIII

The Trustee may vote any stock constituting a part of
the trust estate for an individual Trustee as an officer or
director, or both, of any corporation in which the Trustee holds
stock hereunder, and any individual Trustee may personally re-
ceive and retain compensation for services rendered to any cor-
poration, even though the rendering of such services resulted
from the fact that the Trustee held stock hereunder in such
corporation.

- 14 -

ARTICLE IX

The Trustee is hereby relieved from the duty to make
any inventory or accounting to any court, and any and all statu-
tory requirements concerning accountings by trustees are hereby
waived; provided, however, that any court of competent juris-
diction may, upon the application of any beneficiary or some
person in his or her behalf, require a formal accounting by the
Trustee. The Trustee shall keep accurate books of account,
however, of all transactions pertaining to the trust estate,
showing all disbursements, charges for her services as Trustee
hereunder, receipts of principal and income, and all investments
and changes of investments. Those books shall be open at all
reasonable times for inspection by any beneficiary then having
the right to receive distribution of net income or principal.
The Trustee, upon request, shall render a statement to each such
beneficiary, showing all receipts and disbursements of income
and principal and all charges of investments since the last re-
port, and showing all investments then held as part of the trust
estate, but the Trustee shall not be required to render such
statements more frequently than semi-annually.

ARTICLE X

Whenever more than one Trustee is acting hereunder,
any Trustee may at any time or times give to his or her or its
co-Trustees, or any one or more of them, a power of attorney to
perform any and all acts in relation to the trust estate that
he or she or it might perform if personally present, regardless
of whether such acts involve the exercise of discretion; provided,
however, that no beneficiary acting as a Trustee hereunder shall

- 15 -

have the power, under a power of attorney from his or her co-
Trustees or otherwise, to make or to participate in making any
determination which any of the provisions of this Trust Agreement
prohibit such beneficiary from making.

ARTICLE XI

At all times during the existence of this trust, any
and all acts done, decisions made and instruments executed by a
majority of the Trustees then acting hereunder shall be as
valid and effectual as though done by all of the Trustees then
acting hereunder. A Trustee not concurring therein shall not
be liable for any act done, decision made, or instrument exe-
cuted by a majority of the Trustees.

ARTICLE XII

Whenever a corporate Trustee is acting hereunder, the
Trustee is authorized to permit the corporate Trustee to have
custody of all property constituting the trust estate and the
Trustee is authorized to deposit trust funds with the banking
department of the corporate Trustee. Under no circumstance
shall the Trustee purchase as an investment for the trust estate
any securities or other investments from the corporate Trustee
or from any bond department, investment company, or other af-
filiated or subsidiary corporation operated by it for its own

tained in this Article XIV shall prevent the Settlor's daughter,

Re: 
Judge Read Ambler Statement of Economic Interests 
Dear Ms. Martin: 
This letter summarizes the sUbstance of our recent 
telephone conversations regarding whether and to what extent 
Judge Read Ambler should report income from and investments held 
by trusts created by his maternal grandparents. 
Judge Ambler 
spoke with you and wrote to you previously, and, following your 
direction, filed his annual statement with annotations indicating 
that he was looking into what should be reported regarding trust 
income or assets. 
This letter also presents for your review our conclusions on 
two additional issues: 
1) the question of what percentage 
interest a purely discretionary beneficiary has in a trust, and 
2) the application of the reporting requirements to loans from 
family trusts.
sections.conclusion:
of Discussions 
In our initial telephone conversation I explained that 
Judge Ambler is a purely discretionary current beneficiary of a 
trust created by his maternal grandmother, Josabel L. Read, and 
that the Judge has no irrevocable future interest in the trust. 
The Judge's mother is the principal current beneficiary of the 
trust, and Judge Ambler is a current beneficiary only to the 
extent that the trustee is granted the purely discretionary 
authority to make distributions to him under a provision for 
discretionary distributions to members of a class including the 
testator's grandchildren. 
Last year was the only year in which 
the trust made a distribution to the Judge under this 
discretionary power. 
Regarding future interests, the trust 
grants the Judge's mother a broad power to appoint who should 
receive the trust assets upon her death, and only if that power 


GRAY, CARY, AMES & FRYE 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
April 10, 1989  
of appointment is not exercised would Judge Ambler receive any of 
the trust principal. 
A similar trust primarily for the benefit of the Judge's 
mother was created by his maternal grandfather, Elbert Read, but 
Judge Ambler has even less of an interest in that trust. 
The 
Elbert Read trust does not include a similar discretionary 
distribution provision for grandchildren under which the Judge 
could receive distributions currently, and he would receive trust 
assets in the future again only if his mother did not exercise a 
power of appointment granted to her under the terms of the trust. 
We discussed the application of Fair Political Practices 
Commission Regulation section 18234 to the Judge's situation. 
Under that regulation, a filer must report the pro rata share of 
interests in real property, sources of income and investments of 
a trust if he has a direct, indirect or beneficial interest in 
the trust of ten percent or greater. 
The regulation specifies 
that a filer who is not the maker of a trust has an interest in a 
trust if he is a beneficiary who presently receives income or has 
an irrevocable future right to receive income or principal. 
The 
regulation does not address the question of how to determine the 
percentage of such an interest. 
You said that you would speak to your division chief about 
these issues, and in a subsequent telephone conversation you 
reported the following as the proper application of the 
regulations to Judge Ambler's situation: 
Since another person 
has the power to determine the distributees and the Judge has no 
irrevocable future interest, there is no general requirement that 
the Judge report any interest in the trusts in his annual 
statement. 
However, the Judge would be considered a current 
beneficiary of the trust for any year in v:hich he actually 
received a distribution. 
Therefore, for last year he must report 
the income from the trust. 
He must also report the trust's 
investments for that year if he has an interest in the trust of 
ten percent or greater. 
However, even in that instance he must 
report only interests in real property located in California, 
sources of trust income in California, and investments in 
businesses located in or doing business in California. 
In one 
conversation you explained that trust holdings in stock in 
companies doing business in California must be reported even if 
the stock is traded on a national exchange, but that any dividend 
income from such stock would have to be reported only if the 
Judge's pro rata share of a company's dividends exceeded $10,000 
during the reporting period. 


GRAY, CARY, AM ES & FRYE 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
April 10, 1989  
We discussed that it was possible that there would be no 
such California holdings since the trust that made the 
distribution is an Iowa trust that in the past held Iowa real 
estate, but we agreed that it would be necessary to determine the 
nature of the trust's current holdings if the Judge's interest 
passed the ten percent test. 
I asked how to determine the percentage interest of a purely 
discretionary beneficiary. 
After checking with your division 
chief, you reported that the Commission cannot advise on 
determining the percentage of ownership (the term used in the 
statute) of a trust, and that the trustee should be consulted in 
that regard. 
I suggested that the most logical conclusion is 
that a purely discretionary beneficiary has a zero percent 
interest in the trust, and you said that you would see if your 
division chief had any comment on that. 
In a later conversation 
you confirmed that the Commission cannot advise about percentage 
interests in trusts and that we should consult the trustee. 
Percentage Interest in Trust 
We have consulted with the trustee of the trust that made a 
distribution to Judge Ambler last year. 
That trustee is the 
Judge's mother. 
We have also analyzed the situation further 
ourselves. 
We have still concluded that, since Judge Ambler has 
no definite or irrevocable interest in either income or principal 
of the trust, the most logical conclusion is that he has a zero 
percent ownership interest in it. 
Therefore, since all 
requirements for reporting trust assets and income apply only if 
the 10 percent interest threshold is met, it appears to us that 
Judge Ambler would not be required to report those items even for 
a year in "'Thich he received a distributicr. from the trust. 
Loan From Trust 
It has come to my attention since our last telephone 
conversation that Judge Ambler has received loans from both 
trusts and that both loans are still outstanding. 
Reviewing the 
instructions for Schedule E for loans received or outstanding 
during the reporting period, it appears that the loans from the 
Judge's grandparents' trusts probably are not covered by the 
reporting requirement. 
Under the instructions, loans from 
relatives -
including parents and grandparents - are not required 
to be reported. 
That instruction offers the exception that a 
loan from any such person is reportable if the person is acting 


GRAY, CARY, AMES & FRYE 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
April 10, 1989  
as an intermediary or agent for any person not covered by that 
provision. 
The loans from the trust are in effect loans from either 
Judge Ambler's mother or from his grandparents. 
If the loan is 
regarded as being from the person who established the trust, then 
those loans would be from the Judge's grandparents. 
This would 
seem to be the more logical interpretation in light of the policy 
to disregard intermediaries that is implicit in the exclusion 
described above for loans using a family member as an 
intermediary or agent. 
However, if the loan is not regarded as 
having been made indirectly from the Judge's grandparents, then 
it would be from the Judge's mother as trustee, and the loan 
would thus also not be required to be reported. 
Please let me know your and the Commission's reactions to 
our conclusions regarding the zero percentage ownership of the 
trust and the exclusion from the reporting requirement of loans 
from a family trust. 
Also, please contact me at your earliest convenience if you 
have any corrections to make to this summary of our telephone 
conversations. 
Thank you very much for your assistance in this matter.

Re: 
Judge Read Ambler Statement of Economic Interests 
Dear Ms. Martin: 
This letter summarizes the sUbstance of our recent 
telephone conversations regarding whether and to what extent 
Judge Read Ambler should report income from and investments held 
by trusts created by his maternal grandparents. 
Judge Ambler 
spoke with you and wrote to you previously, and, following your 
direction, filed his annual statement with annotations indicating 
that he was looking into what should be reported regarding trust 
income or assets. 
This letter also presents for your review our conclusions on 
two additional issues: 
1) the question of what percentage 
interest a purely discretionary beneficiary has in a trust, and 
2) the application of the reporting requirements to loans from 
family trusts.

CONCLUSION: of Discussions 
In our initial telephone conversation I explained that 
Judge Ambler is a purely discretionary current beneficiary of a 
trust created by his maternal grandmother, Josabel L. Read, and 
that the Judge has no irrevocable future interest in the trust. 
The Judge's mother is the principal current beneficiary of the 
trust, and Judge Ambler is a current beneficiary only to the 
extent that the trustee is granted the purely discretionary 
authority to make distributions to him under a provision for 
discretionary distributions to members of a class including the 
testator's grandchildren. 
Last year was the only year in which 
the trust made a distribution to the Judge under this 
discretionary power. 
Regarding future interests, the trust 
grants the Judge's mother a broad power to appoint who should 
receive the trust assets upon her death, and only if that power 


GRAY, CARY, AMES & FRYE 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
April 10, 1989  
of appointment is not exercised would Judge Ambler receive any of 
the trust principal. 
A similar trust primarily for the benefit of the Judge's 
mother was created by his maternal grandfather, Elbert Read, but 
Judge Ambler has even less of an interest in that trust. 
The 
Elbert Read trust does not include a similar discretionary 
distribution provision for grandchildren under which the Judge 
could receive distributions currently, and he would receive trust 
assets in the future again only if his mother did not exercise a 
power of appointment granted to her under the terms of the trust. 
We discussed the application of Fair Political Practices 
Commission Regulation section 18234 to the Judge's situation. 
Under that regulation, a filer must report the pro rata share of 
interests in real property, sources of income and investments of 
a trust if he has a direct, indirect or beneficial interest in 
the trust of ten percent or greater. 
The regulation specifies 
that a filer who is not the maker of a trust has an interest in a 
trust if he is a beneficiary who presently receives income or has 
an irrevocable future right to receive income or principal. 
The 
regulation does not address the question of how to determine the 
percentage of such an interest. 
You said that you would speak to your division chief about 
these issues, and in a subsequent telephone conversation you 
reported the following as the proper application of the 
regulations to Judge Ambler's situation: 
Since another person 
has the power to determine the distributees and the Judge has no 
irrevocable future interest, there is no general requirement that 
the Judge report any interest in the trusts in his annual 
statement. 
However, the Judge would be considered a current 
beneficiary of the trust for any year in v:hich he actually 
received a distribution. 
Therefore, for last year he must report 
the income from the trust. 
He must also report the trust's 
investments for that year if he has an interest in the trust of 
ten percent or greater. 
However, even in that instance he must 
report only interests in real property located in California, 
sources of trust income in California, and investments in 
businesses located in or doing business in California. 
In one 
conversation you explained that trust holdings in stock in 
companies doing business in California must be reported even if 
the stock is traded on a national exchange, but that any dividend 
income from such stock would have to be reported only if the 
Judge's pro rata share of a company's dividends exceeded $10,000 
during the reporting period. 


GRAY, CARY, AM ES & FRYE 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
April 10, 1989  
We discussed that it was possible that there would be no 
such California holdings since the trust that made the 
distribution is an Iowa trust that in the past held Iowa real 
estate, but we agreed that it would be necessary to determine the 
nature of the trust's current holdings if the Judge's interest 
passed the ten percent test. 
I asked how to determine the percentage interest of a purely 
discretionary beneficiary. 
After checking with your division 
chief, you reported that the Commission cannot advise on 
determining the percentage of ownership (the term used in the 
statute) of a trust, and that the trustee should be consulted in 
that regard. 
I suggested that the most logical conclusion is 
that a purely discretionary beneficiary has a zero percent 
interest in the trust, and you said that you would see if your 
division chief had any comment on that. 
In a later conversation 
you confirmed that the Commission cannot advise about percentage 
interests in trusts and that we should consult the trustee. 
Percentage Interest in Trust 
We have consulted with the trustee of the trust that made a 
distribution to Judge Ambler last year. 
That trustee is the 
Judge's mother. 
We have also analyzed the situation further 
ourselves. 
We have still concluded that, since Judge Ambler has 
no definite or irrevocable interest in either income or principal 
of the trust, the most logical conclusion is that he has a zero 
percent ownership interest in it. 
Therefore, since all 
requirements for reporting trust assets and income apply only if 
the 10 percent interest threshold is met, it appears to us that 
Judge Ambler would not be required to report those items even for 
a year in "'Thich he received a distributicr. from the trust. 
Loan From Trust 
It has come to my attention since our last telephone 
conversation that Judge Ambler has received loans from both 
trusts and that both loans are still outstanding. 
Reviewing the 
instructions for Schedule E for loans received or outstanding 
during the reporting period, it appears that the loans from the 
Judge's grandparents' trusts probably are not covered by the 
reporting requirement. 
Under the instructions, loans from 
relatives -
including parents and grandparents - are not required 
to be reported. 
That instruction offers the exception that a 
loan from any such person is reportable if the person is acting 


GRAY, CARY, AMES & FRYE 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
April 10, 1989  
as an intermediary or agent for any person not covered by that 
provision. 
The loans from the trust are in effect loans from either 
Judge Ambler's mother or from his grandparents. 
If the loan is 
regarded as being from the person who established the trust, then 
those loans would be from the Judge's grandparents. 
This would 
seem to be the more logical interpretation in light of the policy 
to disregard intermediaries that is implicit in the exclusion 
described above for loans using a family member as an 
intermediary or agent. 
However, if the loan is not regarded as 
having been made indirectly from the Judge's grandparents, then 
it would be from the Judge's mother as trustee, and the loan 
would thus also not be required to be reported. 
Please let me know your and the Commission's reactions to 
our conclusions regarding the zero percentage ownership of the 
trust and the exclusion from the reporting requirement of loans 
from a family trust. 
Also, please contact me at your earliest convenience if you 
have any corrections to make to this summary of our telephone 
conversations. 
Thank you very much for your assistance in this matter.

READ, of Shenandoah, Iowa, hereinafter called the "Settlor," and the Settlor's daughter, MARION R. AMBLER, of Waco, Texas, hereinafter called the "Trustee," W I T N E S S E T H: WHEREAS, on June 1, 1952, the Settlor and the Trustee entered into a certain revocable Trust Agreement for the benefit of the beneficiaries therein named, said Agreement being known as "The Josabel L. Read Trust," and WHEREAS, Article XV of said Trust Agreement provided in part as follows: "Settlor • • • declares that this trust shall be revocable at will and that Settlor shall have full power • • • (b) at any time or from time to time by an instrument in writing delivered during the Settlor's lifetime to the Trustee, to terminate this Agreement in whole or in part, or to modify or amend it, provided, that in the case of modification, or amendment, the duties, powers and liabilities of the Trustee shall not be charged without their written consent • • •," and WHEREAS, the Settlor desires to amend the terms of said Trust Agreement and the Trustee hereby consents to said amendment; NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the premises and pursuant to the right of amendment set forth in said Trust Agreement, it is mutually agreed by and between the Settlor and the Trustee as follows: ITEM I Articles III, IV, V, VI, VII, VIII, IX, X, XI, XII, XIII, XIV, XV, XVI, XVII and XVIII of said Trust Agreement are hereby cancelled and revoked and in lieu thereof the following Articles III, IV, V, VI, VII, VIII, IX, X, XI, XII, XIII, XIV, XV, XVI, XVII, XVIII, XIX, XX and XXI are hereby substituted: ARTICLE III During the lifetime of the Settlor, the trust estate and all income derived therefrom shall be held, administered and distributed as follows: A. The Trustee shall, at convenient intervals but not less frequently than quarter-annually, pay to or use for the benefit of the Settlor all of the net income derived from the trust estate so long as she shall live. Upon the death of the Settlor any accrued or collected but undistributed net income which she would have otherwise been entitled to receive shall not be distributed to the executors or administrators of her estate but instead shall be treated as part of the principal of the trust estate. B. The Settlor shall have the absolute right to with- draw from the trust estate such sum or sums out of the principal thereof as she may designate by written request to the Trustee. If such withdrawals shall exhaust the trust estate, then the trust estate shall terminate upon such exhaustion. Any with- drawals so made by the Settlor shall not be deemed to be advance- ments upon any payments of net income or principal which the Settlor may thereafter become entitled to receive. C. If at
